stemHtml: A pilot is performing flight tests on two airplanes: Airplane 1 and Airplane 2, which has double the mass of Airplane 1. While flying Airplane 1, the pilot performs a steady level turn with 18° angle of bank and notes the radius of turn using external visual cues and/or references. If the pilot performs the same steady level turn at the same TAS with Airplane 2, the required angle bank to maintain the same radius of turn would…

options.a: be greater than 18°, because the centripetal force to maintain the same radius of turn will quadruple.
options.b: be greater than 18°, because the centripetal force to maintain the same radius of the turn will increase by the same proportion.
options.c: be less than 18°, because the centripetal force to maintain the same radius of turn will decrease by the same proportion.
options.d: still be 18°, because the centripetal force to maintain the same radius of the turn will increase by the same proportion.
